vinayaññūnamaggena.meaning: by the path of those who know the Vinaya; by the procedure of one versed in the discipline
vinayaññūnamaggena.summary: This is a compound meaning “by the path/procedure of knowing the Vinaya”.
breakdown: The monastic discipline; the code of training rules.
Path; procedure, method (here instrumental maggena, “by the path”).

vinenti.meaning: they put away; they instruct, train, conquer
breakdown: Root: to lead. vi + √nī, “to lead away”, hence to remove or to train, discipline.
Root: to lead. “To lead away”, hence to remove, and by extension to train, instruct, educate.
Root: to lead; vi + √nī, to remove, put away.

vinicchayamānāpi.meaning: even while investigating; investigation and measuring too

vinipātaṁ.meaning: the downfall; a state of suffering, a place of ruin (where beings fall after death)
breakdown: Falling down (ni “down” + √pat “to fall”). Hence a fall into a lower state of existence, a state of woe.

vinodameva.meaning: just the dispelling; only the removal
vinodameva.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular + enclitic eva
breakdown: Emphatic particle: just, only, indeed. (The final -m of vinodam is sandhi before eva.)

vinodeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of vi + √nud
breakdown: Root: to push, drive. Causative stem node/noda: to cause to be driven away, to dispel.

vinābhāvo.meaning: separation; the state of being separated (from what is dear)
breakdown: State, condition. Together: the state of being apart, separation.

vināseti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of vinassati (vi + √nas)
breakdown: Root: to perish. Causative stem nāse: to cause to perish, to destroy.

vipaccati.meaning: ripens, bears fruit; comes to fruition (of kamma)
vipaccati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, passive of vipacati (vi + √pac)
breakdown: Root: to cook, ripen. The passive stem pacca: to be ripened, to come to fruition.

vipanno.gram: past participle of vipajjati (vi + √pad), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root: to go. vi + √pad, “to go wrong”, to fail.

vipariṇāmadhammaṁ.meaning: subject to change; of a nature to alter and reverse
vipariṇāmadhammaṁ.summary: This is a compound meaning “having change as its nature”, said of all conditioned things.
breakdown: Here: nature, quality — “having the nature of...”.

vipassanā.meaning: insight meditation; the practice of seeing things as they really are; direct experiential insight
breakdown: Prefix: in a special way, distinctly, thoroughly.
Seeing, observing (√pass/√dis, to see). Together: seeing things in a special way — as they really are.
vipassanā.goenka: In this tradition Vipassana is the heart of the Buddha's teaching: the direct, experiential observation of reality within oneself, seeing things as they really are. Klaus writes the romanised form “Vipassana” (capitalised, without diacritics) when referring to the practice and tradition, and the Pāli form vipassanā in textual context. He explicitly equates Vipassana with purification of the mind: “Vipassana for purification of the mind. Only with mental purification does a person become truly established in sīla.” He also speaks of “the calming meditation of Vipassana” and “the magnificent teaching of Vipassana.” It is the stage of paññā in the progression of the path: sīla (morality) → samādhi (concentration) → vipassanā (insight) leading to nibbāna.

vipassanāgabbhaṁ.meaning: the womb of vipassanā; the interior (embryonic stage) of insight
breakdown: Insight; seeing things as they really are.

vippahāya.meaning: having completely abandoned; having given up
breakdown: Prefix: intensive (the pp doubling gives vippa-).

vippamutto.gram: past participle of vippamuccati (vi + pa + √muc), masculine nominative singular

vippasanno.gram: past participle of vippasīdati (vi + pa + √sad), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root: to settle, become clear. vi + pa + √sad: to become perfectly clear, serene.

vippayogopi.meaning: separation too; even being apart (from what is dear)
vippayogopi.summary: From the first noble truth: “separation from what is dear is suffering” (piyehi vippayogo dukkho).
vippayogopi.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular + enclitic pi
breakdown: Combination, association (pa + √yuj, to join). With vi-: dis-association, separation.

vippaṭisārino.gram: adjective (vippaṭisārī), genitive/dative singular or nominative plural
breakdown: Root: to remember. Remembering back (with pain): remorse, regret.
Possessive adjective suffix: one having remorse.
Root: to throb, quiver. Hence to struggle, writhe.

vipphanditavinayakaraṁ.meaning: effecting the removal of struggling; bringing about the abolishment of the struggle of physical and verbal actions
breakdown: Struggling, writhing (past participle of vipphandati used as a noun).
Root: to be great, heap up. vipula: abundant, extensive.

vipākasocanaṁ.meaning: sorrowing over the result; grief at the fruit (of one's deeds)

virahitanti.gram: adjective virahita, accusative singular + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Root: to leave, be separate. virahita: deprived of, without.
Quotation particle (iti), marking the preceding word as cited speech.
Root: to delight, stop. viramati: to abstain, desist.

virattacittāyatike.meaning: with mind detached from future becoming; whose heart is detached regarding the future
breakdown: Belonging to the future (āyati, the future).

viratī.gram: feminine noun (= virati, with metrically lengthened final vowel)
breakdown: Root: to delight, stop; viramati, to abstain. The long -ī is metri causa in verse.

virādhayitvā.meaning: having failed, having missed; having lost
breakdown: Root: to succeed. With vi-: to fail, miss.

virāganissitaṁ.meaning: based on dispassion; given to detachment
breakdown: Dispassion, detachment, fading away of craving.
Based on, depending on, given to (past participle of nissayati).
Passion, craving, colouring (√raj, to colour). virāga: the fading away of passion, detachment.

virāgaṁ detachment vilepana.meaning: virāgaṁ: detachment; vilepana: cosmetics, ointment, unguent
virāgaṁ detachment vilepana.summary: A glossary line covering two separate words: virāgaṁ (detachment) and vilepana (cosmetics).
breakdown: Cosmetics; ointment, unguent applied to the body.

virāgāya.meaning: for detachment; for the sake of dispassion
virāgāya.gram: masculine noun, dative singular of virāga
breakdown: Dative singular ending: for, for the sake of.

visabhāgasantati.meaning: a dissimilar continuity; a different lineage or succession
breakdown: Different, dissimilar (vi + sabhāga, “not having a common part”).

visame.meaning: uneven, disharmonious; in what is uneven (wrong conduct)
breakdown: Even, equal, harmonious. visama: uneven, discordant, unrighteous.

visaṁvādanapurekkhārassa.meaning: of one devoted to deceiving; of one intent on lying
breakdown: Putting in front, deference; being devoted or intent on.

visaṁvādanādhippāyena.meaning: with the intention of deceiving; with intent to lie
breakdown: Desire, intention (the long ā- joins by sandhi: -na + adhi- > -nādhi-).

vissajjesi.meaning: distributed; answered, sent forth; spent, gave away
vissajjesi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of vissajjeti

vissajjeti.meaning: sends forth, sends away; emits, discharges; distributes, answers
vissajjeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of vissajjati (vi + √sajj)

vissaṭṭhaparalokassa.meaning: of one who has dismissed the next world; of one who has given up concern for the world beyond
breakdown: Dismissed, given up (past participle of vissajjati; vi + saṭṭha, pp. of sajati).
World. paraloka: the next world, the world beyond.
Trust, confidence, intimacy (vi + √sas, to breathe; “breathing freely with”).
Adjectival suffix: belonging to, characterised by.

vissāsiko.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular of vissāsika
breakdown: Adjectival suffix: belonging to, characterised by.

visuddhimagga.meaning: the Visuddhimagga (“Path of Purification”), Buddhaghosa's classical commentarial compendium
visuddhimagga.gram: proper noun (title of a text); masculine compound

visārade.gram: adjective, locative singular or vocative/accusative plural
breakdown: Autumnal; figuratively immature, timid. visārada: free from timidity — confident, skilled.
Timid, immature. visārada: free from timidity — confident, self-possessed.

visūkadassanā.meaning: from seeing shows; visiting spectacles and exhibitions (abstaining from which is part of right conduct)

vitakkanamattameva.meaning: just mere thinking; only the mere act of thought
vitakkanamattameva.gram: compound noun, accusative singular + enclitic eva
breakdown: Emphatic particle: just, only. (The -m- is sandhi: mattam + eva.)

vitakkapariyāyapathesu.meaning: in the courses of the succession of thoughts; among the ways of thought-sequences
